summary: Marketing is in full swing with dual campaigns for Asia Start and the new Intelligent China program, while the high-stakes 'China Going-Global' report with IE University is on a tight timeline for a September launch in Singapore.
summaryDetail: The team is executing parallel campaigns for Asia Start (AI and traditional routes) and launching the Intelligent China program for foreigners, with urgent website finalization and SCM planning needed this week. The 'New Chinese Playbook for Going Global' report with IE University is the top priority: Felix must confirm the launch event (Tech in Asia or Tyson) by early July, while the team translates the report (keeping it under 50% of the Chinese original) and drafts op-eds from both schools' leadership. Meanwhile, social media agency selection is expedited (likely Media Minds), and conversion tracking audits for China programs begin, with Joseph providing initial search visibility areas. Upcoming: review of translated articles and knowledge page by July 3, and finalizing the IE report structure and promotion plan.

meetings.title: Marketing Strategy for China Programs
meetings.summary: Team prioritizes Asia Star and Intelligent China programs for marketing, with secondary focus on Berkeley. They plan to audit current conversion tracking and develop content to improve search visibility.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting - June 29
meetings.summary: Team discussed website menu feedback, brochure updates, CES quotes, Asia Start articles, Davos press release, social media agency search, and IE report translation. Decisions made on press release approach and agency direction.

meetings.title: New Chinese Playbook Report Planning
meetings.summary: Team discusses joint report between CKGSB and IE on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, with op-eds from both schools' leadership and industry case studies, aiming for September launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson event in Singapore.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Discussed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional), reviewed results, and introduced a new in-depth program 'Intelligent China' targeting foreigners, with focus on module one in Beijing.

meetings.title: EU-China Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on EU-China relations, focusing on geopolitical tensions, de-risking, and the role of companies versus governments in market operations.

decisions.text: Prioritize Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships initially.
Include Berkeley as a third priority for overseas programs.
Only one press release for Shun Tian Shu, either incorporating 10 points or turning into 10-point article.
Prioritize speed in social media agency selection; likely stick with Media Minds.
IE report translation should not exceed 50% of original Chinese report.
Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE, with joint distribution.
Working title: 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.
Report will include a main research feature by Professor Leeway, plus op-eds from both schools' leadership and industry case studies.
Target launch at either Tech in Asia (Sept 15-16) or Tyson event (Sept 29-30) in Singapore, with digital version ready by early September.
Asia Start will run two parallel campaigns for the first month: AI route (Joseph) and traditional route (Daisy).
Intelligent China program will target foreigners, with module one in Beijing (Dec 1-4).
Use Temu as the second case study for the IE University report instead of halocoko
Place key findings at the beginning of the IE University report
Professor Li Wei confirmed support for the China's New Playbook for Going Global report project
decisions.meetingTitle: Re: 2026年英文报告:  China’s New Playbook for Going Global
